title: Multiply & divide rational expressions: Error analysis
description: Analyze other people's attempts to multiply and divide rational expressions, and identify the mistake they made.
topic_path: Multiply & divide rational expressions: Error analysis

assessment_items.question_content:
Jasper tried to perform the following multiplication and reduce to lowest terms:

$\blueE{\dfrac{4x-8}{x^2-1}} \cdot \maroonE{\dfrac{x^2+3x+2}{x-2}}$

This is his work:

>Rewriting the first factor: $\blueE{\dfrac{4x-8}{x^2-1}} =\dfrac{4(x-2)}{(x+1)(x-1)}$

>Rewriting the second factor: $\maroonE{\dfrac{x^2+3x+2}{x-2}}=\dfrac{(x+1)(x+2)}{x-2}$

>Finding excluded values: $x\neq \pm 1$

>Multiplying and reducing to lowest terms:

>$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\blueE{\dfrac{4x-8}{x^2-1}} \cdot \maroonE{\dfrac{x^2+3x+2}{x-2}}
\\\\
&=\dfrac{4(x-2)}{(x+1)(x-1)}\cdot\dfrac{(x+1)(x+2)}{x-2}
\\\\
&=\dfrac{4\cancel{(x-2)}\cancel{(x+1)}(x+2)}{\cancel{(x+1)}(x-1)\cancel{(x-2)}}
\\\\
&=\dfrac{4(x+2)}{x-1}
\end{align}$

**What mistake did Jasper make?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: He excluded $x=-1$, which doesn't make the denominator in the final expression equal to zero.
He didn't exclude $x=2$, which makes the denominator of the second factor equal to zero.
He didn't rewrite the first factor correctly.
He didn't rewrite the second factor correctly.

assessment_items.question_content:
Vicente tried to perform the following multiplication and reduce to lowest terms:

$\blueE{\dfrac{x^2-36}{x+3}} \cdot \maroonE{\dfrac{2x+6}{x+6}}$

This is his work:

>Rewriting the first factor: $\blueE{\dfrac{x^2-36}{x+3}} =\dfrac{(x+6)(x-6)}{(x+3)}$

>Rewriting the second factor: $\maroonE{\dfrac{2x+6}{x+6}}=\dfrac{2(x+3)}{x+6}$

>Finding excluded values: $x\neq \pm 6$ and $x\neq -3$

>Multiplying and reducing to lowest terms:

>$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\blueE{\dfrac{x^2-36}{x+3}} \cdot \maroonE{\dfrac{2x+6}{x+6}}
\\\\
&=\dfrac{(x+6)(x-6)}{(x+3)}\cdot\dfrac{2(x+3)}{x+6}
\\\\
&=\dfrac{2\cancel{(x+6)}(x-6)\cancel{(x+3)}}{\cancel{(x+3)}\cancel{(x+6)}}
\\\\
&=2(x-6)
\end{align}$

**What mistake did Vicente make?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: He excluded $x=-3$, which doesn't make the final expression undefined.
He excluded $x=6$, which doesn't make the denominator in either expression equal to zero.
He didn't rewrite the first factor correctly.
He didn't rewrite the second factor correctly.

assessment_items.question_content:
Elizabeth tried to perform the following multiplication and reduce to lowest terms:

$\blueE{\dfrac{-3x+24}{x-4}} \cdot \maroonE{\dfrac{x^2-8x+16}{x+7}}$

This is her work:

>Rewriting the first factor: $\blueE{\dfrac{-3x+24}{x-4}} =\dfrac{-3(x-8)}{x-4}$

>Rewriting the second factor: $\maroonE{\dfrac{x^2-8x+16}{x+7}}=\dfrac{(x-4)^2}{x+7}$

>Finding excluded values: $x\neq -7$, $x\neq 4$, and $x\neq 8$

>Multiplying and reducing to lowest terms:

>$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\blueE{\dfrac{-3x+24}{x-4}} \cdot \maroonE{\dfrac{x^2-8x+16}{x+7}}
\\\\
&=\dfrac{-3(x-8)}{x-4}\cdot\dfrac{(x-4)^2}{x+7}
\\\\
&=\dfrac{-3(x-8)\cancel{(x-4)}(x-4)}{\cancel{(x-4)}(x+7)}
\\\\
&=\dfrac{-3(x-8)(x-4)}{x+7}
\end{align}$

**What mistake did Elizabeth make?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: She excluded $x=4$, which doesn't make the final expression undefined.
She excluded $x=8$, which doesn't make the denominator in either expression equal to zero.
She didn't rewrite the first factor correctly.
She didn't rewrite the second factor correctly.
She excluded $x=-4$, which only makes the numerators equal to zero.
She didn't exclude $x=-5$, which makes the denominator of the dividend equal to zero.
She didn't rewrite the dividend correctly.
She didn't rewrite the divisor correctly.

assessment_items.question_content:
Sharon tried to perform the following division and reduce to lowest terms:

$\blueE{\dfrac{x^2-49}{x+5}} \div\maroonE{\dfrac{x-7}{x^2+6x+5}}$

This is her work:

>Rewriting the dividend: $\blueE{\dfrac{x^2-49}{x+5}} =\dfrac{(x+7)(x-7)}{x+5}$

>Rewriting the divisor: $\maroonE{\dfrac{x-7}{x^2+6x+5}}=\dfrac{x-7}{(x+1)(x+5)}$

>Finding excluded values: $x\neq \pm7$, $x\neq -5$, and $x\neq -1$

>Dividing and reducing to lowest terms:

>$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\blueE{\dfrac{x^2-49}{x+5}} \div\maroonE{\dfrac{x-7}{x^2+6x+5}}
\\\\
&=\dfrac{(x+7)(x-7)}{x+5}\div\dfrac{x-7}{(x+1)(x+5)}
\\\\
&=\dfrac{(x+7)(x-7)}{x+5}\cdot\dfrac{(x+1)(x+5)}{x-7}
\\\\
&=\dfrac{(x+7)\cancel{(x-7)}(x+1)\cancel{(x+5)}}{\cancel{(x+5)}\cancel{(x-7)}}
\\\\
&=(x+7)(x+1)
\end{align}$

**What mistake did Sharon make?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: She excluded $x=-7$, which only makes the numerator of the dividend equal to zero.
She excluded some values of $x$, although the final quotient is defined for all numbers.
She didn't rewrite the dividend correctly.
She didn't rewrite the divisor correctly.
He excluded $x=-2$, which doesn't make the final expression undefined.
He didn't exclude $x=-6$, which makes the numerator of the divisor equal to zero.
He didn't rewrite the dividend correctly.
He didn't rewrite the divisor correctly.

assessment_items.question_content:
Rutendo tried to perform the following multiplication and reduce to lowest terms:

$\blueE{\dfrac{-3x+15}{2x+16}} \cdot \maroonE{\dfrac{x-7}{x^2-25}}$

This is his work:

>Rewriting the first factor: $\blueE{\dfrac{-3x+15}{2x+16}} =\dfrac{-3(x+5)}{2(x+8)}$

>Rewriting the second factor: $\maroonE{\dfrac{x-7}{x^2-25}}=\dfrac{x-7}{(x+5)(x-5)}$

>Finding excluded values: $x\neq \pm 5$ and $x\neq -8$

>Multiplying and reducing to lowest terms:

>$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\blueE{\dfrac{-3x+15}{2x+16}} \cdot \maroonE{\dfrac{x-7}{x^2-25}}
\\\\
&=\dfrac{-3(x+5)}{2(x+8)}\cdot\dfrac{x-7}{(x+5)(x-5)}
\\\\
&=\dfrac{-3\cancel{(x+5)}(x-7)}{2(x+8)\cancel{(x+5)}(x-5)}
\\\\
&=\dfrac{-3(x-7)}{2(x+8)(x-5)}
\end{align}$

**What mistake did Rutendo make?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: He excluded $x=-5$, which doesn't make the final expression undefined.
He didn't exclude $x=7$, which makes the numerator of the second factor equal to zero.
He didn't rewrite the first factor correctly.
He didn't rewrite the second factor correctly.

assessment_items.question_content:
Chloe tried to perform the following multiplication and reduce to lowest terms:

$\blueE{\dfrac{x+4}{2x-6}} \cdot \maroonE{\dfrac{-4x-12}{x^2+7x+12}}$

This is her work:

>Rewriting the first factor: $\blueE{\dfrac{x+4}{2x-6}} =\dfrac{x+4}{2(x-3)}$

>Rewriting the second factor: $\maroonE{\dfrac{-4x-12}{x^2+7x+12}}=\dfrac{-4(x-3)}{(x+3)(x+4)}$

>Finding excluded values: $x\neq \pm 3$ and $x\neq -4$

>Multiplying and reducing to lowest terms:

>$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\blueE{\dfrac{x+4}{2x-6}} \cdot \maroonE{\dfrac{-4x-12}{x^2+7x+12}}
\\\\
&=\dfrac{x+4}{2(x-3)}\cdot\dfrac{-4(x-3)}{(x+3)(x+4)}
\\\\
&=\dfrac{-2\cdot\cancel 2\cancel{(x+4)}\cancel{(x-3)}}{\cancel 2\cancel{(x-3)}(x+3)\cancel{(x+4)}}
\\\\
&=\dfrac{-2}{(x+3)}
\end{align}$

**What mistake did Chloe make?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: She excluded $x=-4$, which doesn't make the final expression undefined.
She didn't exclude $x=0$.
She didn't rewrite the first factor correctly.
She didn't rewrite the second factor correctly.

assessment_items.question_content:
Afia tried to perform the following multiplication and reduce to lowest terms:

$\blueE{\dfrac{x^2-4x+3}{x-6}} \cdot \maroonE{\dfrac{x-8}{x^2-5x+6}}$

This is her work:

>Rewriting the first factor: $\blueE{\dfrac{x^2-4x+3}{x-6}} =\dfrac{(x-2)^2}{x-6}$

>Rewriting the second factor: $\maroonE{\dfrac{x-8}{x^2-5x+6}}=\dfrac{x-8}{(x-2)(x-3)}$

>Finding excluded values: $x\neq 2$, $x \neq 3$, and $x\neq 6$

>Multiplying and reducing to lowest terms:

>$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\blueE{\dfrac{x^2-4x+3}{x-6}} \cdot \maroonE{\dfrac{x-8}{x^2-5x+6}}
\\\\
&=\dfrac{(x-2)^2}{x-6}\cdot\dfrac{x-8}{(x-2)(x-3)}
\\\\
&=\dfrac{(x-2)\cancel{(x-2)}(x-8)}{(x-6)\cancel{(x-2)}(x-3)}
\\\\
&=\dfrac{(x-2)(x-8)}{(x-6)(x-3)}
\end{align}$

**What mistake did Afia make?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: She excluded $x=2$, which doesn't make the final expression undefined.
She didn't exclude $x=8$, which makes the numerator of the second factor equal to zero.
She didn't rewrite the first factor correctly.
She didn't rewrite the second factor correctly.
She excluded $x=-5$, which only makes the numerators of the dividend and the divisor equal to zero.
She excluded $x=-3$, which doesn't make the denominator in the final quotient equal to zero.
She didn't rewrite the dividend correctly.
She didn't rewrite the divisor correctly.

assessment_items.question_content:
Kelsey tried to perform the following division and reduce to lowest terms:

$\blueE{\dfrac{x^2+12x+35}{x+1}} \div\maroonE{\dfrac{x+6}{x^2+2x+1}}$

This is her work:

>Rewriting the dividend: $\blueE{\dfrac{x^2+12x+35}{x+1}} =\dfrac{(x+6)^2}{x+1}$

>Rewriting the divisor: $\maroonE{\dfrac{x+6}{x^2+2x+1}}=\dfrac{x+6}{(x+1)^2}$

>Finding excluded values: $x\neq -6$ and $x\neq -1$

>Dividing and reducing to lowest terms:

>$\begin{align}
&\phantom{=}\blueE{\dfrac{x^2+12x+35}{x+1}} \div\maroonE{\dfrac{x+6}{x^2+2x+1}}
\\\\
&=\dfrac{(x+6)^2}{x+1}\div\dfrac{x+6}{(x+1)^2}
\\\\
&=\dfrac{(x+6)^2}{x+1}\cdot\dfrac{(x+1)^2}{x+6}
\\\\
&=\dfrac{(x+6)\cancel{(x+6)}\cancel{(x+1)}(x+1)}{\cancel{(x+1)}\cancel{(x+6)}}
\\\\
&=(x+6)(x+1)
\end{align}$

**What mistake did Kelsey make?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: She excluded $x=-6$, which only makes the numerators of the dividend and the divisor equal to zero.
She didn't exclude $x=0$.
She didn't rewrite the dividend correctly.
She didn't rewrite the divisor correctly.
He excluded $x=1$, which only makes the numerator of the divisor equal to zero.
He excluded $x=-1$, which doesn't make the denominator in the final quotient equal to zero.
He didn't rewrite the dividend correctly.
He didn't rewrite the divisor correctly.
